title: People
text: This page introduces the AD-Genomics-Cardiff team. Replace placeholder entries with confirmed names, roles, biographies, and links when those details are ready.

title: People
text:
Team overview
AD-Genomics-Cardiff is part of UK DRI Cardiff and focuses on integrative analysis of genetic and multi-omics data in Alzheimer’s disease research.



Team scope

Integrative analysis across genetics and molecular data
Methods spanning GWAS, polygenic risk, sequencing, and downstream interpretation
Collaborative work across computational, statistical, and biological domains

title: Home
text:
UK DRI Cardiff team hub for Alzheimer’s disease genomics

AD-Genomics-Cardiff is a UKDRI Cardiff team focused on integrative analysis of genetic and multi-omics data for Alzheimer’s disease research. The site brings together our research themes, projects, outputs, and team information in one place, with room to add datasets, methods, and collaborative resources over time.
UK Dementia Research Institute at Cardiff University


 Team page ·  GitHub ·  Contact ·  UK DRI Cardiff

title: Home
text:
Research themes


GWAS and polygenic risk
Understanding inherited susceptibility to Alzheimer’s disease through genome-wide association studies and polygenic modelling.


Long-read sequencing and variant calling
Improving detection and interpretation of genetic variation using long-read data and robust analytical workflows.


Pathway and functional analyses
Linking genomic signals to biological mechanisms through integrative downstream analysis across molecular layers.

title: Projects
text:
This page highlights current AD-Genomics-Cardiff workstreams. Specific project names, datasets, and outputs can be added as they are ready to share publicly.



Integrative genetics workstream
Focus: Connecting Alzheimer’s disease risk loci to downstream biological mechanisms using genetic and multi-omics data.
Current activity: Placeholder summary for cohort integration, statistical analysis, and interpretation work led by the team.
Methods: GWAS, polygenic risk modelling, pathway analysis, and functional follow-up.
Evidence: - Placeholder project page or repository link - Placeholder report, preprint, or poster link - Placeholder dataset or methods resource




Sequencing and variant interpretation workstream
Focus: Using long-read sequencing and variant-calling workflows to improve resolution of Alzheimer’s disease-relevant genomic variation.
Current activity: Placeholder summary for pipeline development, benchmarking, quality control, and biological interpretation.
Methods: Long-read sequencing, variant calling, annotation, and comparative analyses.
